# codex-swarm v0.6.1

`v0.6.1` makes live messaging truthful for Codex tasks whose active app-server
connection is owned by Codex Desktop or another external Codex host.

## Highlights

- `cs message --json` returns a `native_steering` request containing the exact
  ledger path, host, thread, turn, prompt, message ID, and delivery ID.
- The owning Codex host can inject that prompt with its native task-message
  tool, then record verified SQLite readback with `cs message
  confirm-steered`.
- Failed native injection remains queued and auditable through `cs message
  steering-failed`; confirmation is guarded by worker, thread, and turn and is
  idempotent after success.
- Worker records and daemon summaries retain runtime owner, host ID, thread ID,
  and turn ID. `cs show` exposes the same runtime identity for operators.
- `cs`-owned app-server workers continue to consume queued messages over the
  connection they already own.

## Runtime contract

An in-flight turn is scoped to the app-server connection that owns it. A second
app-server process can resume thread history but cannot steer that active turn.
codex-swarm therefore never marks an externally owned delivery as steered until
the owning Codex host reports a successful native injection.

The message remains durable in the machine-global SQLite ledger throughout the
bridge. Replaying the original idempotency key returns the same pending bridge
request until it is confirmed.
